methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Stratolaunch is a private-stage space and aerospace company competing within a defined but limited peer universe of 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX and Sierra Space. The sector median P/S multiple across public space companies stands at 25.8x, reflecting the capital-intensity and long development cycles characteristic of the industry.

A peer-median valuation approach is not applicable here. While 11 same-sector peers exist, filtering by stage, maturity tier, and business model yields fewer than three datapoints in any meaningful subgroup, making any derived median statistically unreliable and potentially misleading. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be executed because Stratolaunch's revenue is not disclosed—a common constraint when valuing private-stage frontier companies. The high sector P/S median of 25.8x is directionally informative but cannot be operationalized without revenue data.

This valuation presents a structural challenge: both triangulation methods that would normally apply to a capital-intensive aerospace business fail due to data gaps. Without disclosed revenue or a sufficiently dense peer cohort at equivalent stage and scale, quantitative synthesis to a point estimate or range is not defensible from the data provided. Any valuation would require either target revenue disclosure, primary market research on comparable deal terms, or a shift to cost-of-capital or discounted-cash-flow methods—none of which are present in the current dataset. The key uncertainty is operational runway and path to revenue generation relative to the company's capital structure.
